meta.description: Module 4 — News-to-Barrels AI. Converts market-moving news into estimated barrel impact with an explicit impact chain and confidence grade. SAMPLE events for demonstration. Update per docs/DAILY_UPDATE_GUIDE.md.
meta.methodology: Pipeline: (1) ingest the event from public reporting; (2) classify it (outage, policy, sanction, weather, infrastructure); (3) size the affected capacity in kb/d from public capacity data; (4) map it through the barrel chain — crude demand, product supply, cracks, differentials, freight; (5) grade confidence by how well the capacity figure and duration are corroborated, and state what would change the assessment. AI-assisted desk analysis reviewed by an editor — estimates for information, never trading signals.

events.headline: Refinery outage in Rotterdam

impacts.effect: Lower crude demand in NW Europe — ~400 kb/d of crude runs lost while the unit is down.
Higher diesel imports into ARA to replace lost production; East-of-Suez and US Gulf cargoes bid.
Wider diesel crack in Europe — supply loss into an already import-dependent market.
Potential weakness in Forties/Ekofisk differentials as a natural local buyer of these grades steps away.

events.confidence_note: Outage confirmed by operator; capacity figure from public refinery data. Duration is the uncertain variable — repairs of this type have ranged 2–8 weeks.
events.watch: Operator restart guidance; ARA diesel stock draws; Forties differential vs Dated. A restart inside two weeks halves the diesel-crack impact.

events.headline: OPEC+ ministers signal faster unwind of voluntary cuts

impacts.effect: Adds ~250 kb/d per month from October — roughly +1.5 mb/d by end-Q1 if the full schedule runs.
Back of the curve absorbs the supply first — pressures the rich Brent 6-month spread flagged in Module 3.
Returning barrels are mostly medium sour — narrows the sour premium, widens Brent–Dubai.
Paradoxically supportive of the risk premium: less spare cushion once barrels return.

events.confidence_note: Ministerial signalling only — no formal decision. OPEC+ has deferred announced unwinds twice before when prices softened.
events.watch: The formal ministerial statement; Saudi OSPs (a cut confirms barrels are really coming); compliance data for early over-production.

events.headline: Tropical system tracking toward US Gulf production corridor
events.affected_capacity: up to 1.5 mb/d offshore + 2.0 mb/d refining in cone
events.duration_estimate: Landfall window 5–7 days; shut-ins typically 3–10 days

impacts.effect: Precautionary platform shut-ins remove offshore barrels — bullish WTI prompt if evacuations begin.
Refinery shutdowns cut crude demand — a landfall on refining centres is crude-bearish, product-bullish.
Gasoline and diesel cracks widen on lost refinery output, especially mid-driving-season.
Port closures delay loadings; the existing US Gulf vessel queue (Module 2) lengthens.

events.confidence_note: Track and intensity still wide. The net crude effect flips sign depending on whether production or refining takes the hit — this is a two-sided event.
events.watch: NHC track updates; operator evacuation notices; LOOP and Corpus Christi port status.

events.headline: New sanctions designations target shadow-fleet tankers
events.capacity_type: sanctioned exports at risk of disruption
events.duration_estimate: Structural — enforcement cycle measured in months

impacts.effect: Up to ~300 kb/d of sanctioned exports face friction finding vessels and buyers — effective supply tightens.
More STS transfers and AIS gaps as the trade adapts — consistent with the 1.8σ dark-AIS anomaly in Module 2.
Designations shrink the usable fleet — supportive for compliant VLCC owners (see Module 3 freight note).
Asian refiners substituting away from discounted barrels bid up compliant sour grades.

events.confidence_note: Designations are public and specific; the offset is the shadow trade's demonstrated ability to re-route within weeks. Net effect likely smaller than the headline figure.
events.watch: Iranian/Russian export loadings over the next 4 weeks (Module 2); the discount on sanctioned grades — a widening discount means enforcement is biting.
